truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against the 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 found several vulnerabilities including format string injection, stack buffer overflow, path traversal, and command injection (validate_config), but misclassified severities and CWEs for some findings and missed many other true vulnerabilities.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: {"title": "Format String Vulnerability in log_debug_message", "vulnerability_type": "Format String Vulnerability", "cwe": "CWE-134", "severity": "High", "confidence": 0.9, "file": "enhanced_calc.cpp", "line_start": 237, "line_end": 238, "function_or_symbol": "log_debug_message", "evidence": "        std::string active_format = runtime_log_format();\n        printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);",
Items.Notes: The previous answer identified the format string vulnerability with CWE-134 and the correct code snippet, but listed severity as High instead of Critical.
The previous answer did not mention integer overflow in factorial computation or CWE-190.
The previous answer did not mention use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup or CWE-416.
The previous answer identified command injection via config validation with CWE-78, but listed severity as High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: {"title": "Stack Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression", "vulnerability_type": "Buffer Overflow", "cwe": "CWE-120", "severity": "High", "confidence": 1.0, "file": "enhanced_calc.cpp", "line_start": 831, "line_end": 833, "function_or_symbol": "evaluate_expression", "evidence": "            if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {\n                strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());\n            } else {",
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the stack buffer overflow with CWE-120 and severity High, with the correct code snippet.
The previous answer did not mention race condition in thread-pool counter or CWE-362 with volatile long long.

The previous answer identified the emergency override backdoor logic but classified it as Authentication Bypass (CWE-307) with severity Critical, whereas the true vulnerability is Logic Bomb in Authentication (CWE-511) with severity High.
The previous answer correctly identified path traversal with CWE-22 and severity Medium, with the correct code snippet.
The previous answer did not mention heap overflow in expression parser or CWE-122 with buffer_size_ = 256 and strcpy(expression_buffer_).
The previous answer did not identify hardcoded administrator credentials as a distinct vulnerability with CWE-798.
The previous answer did not mention weak PRNG for session tokens or CWE-338.
The previous answer did not mention thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging or CWE-362 with static std::tm cached_time.
The previous answer did not mention insecure temporary file handling or CWE-377 with TEMP/TMPDIR and supercalc_emergency.log.
The previous answer did not mention persistent authentication state or CWE-613.
The previous answer did not mention unhandled exception in variable parsing or CWE-754 with std::stod(tokens[3]).
The previous answer did not mention integer underflow in memory-pool block split or CWE-191 with SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES.
The previous answer did not mention unbounded recursion in parser or CWE-674 with parse_expression, parse_factor, PAREN_OPEN.
The previous answer did not mention data race in result cache or CWE-362 with result_cache_, evaluate_cached, cache_mutex_, thread_pool_->enqueue.
The previous answer identified the code snippet for TOCTOU but labeled it as Path Traversal (CWE-22) rather than TOCTOU in Config Loading (CWE-367).
The previous answer did not mention integer division by zero in command routing or CWE-369 with calculate_route_score and 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length).
